(also **Yehuda Fuchs**, [Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): יהודה פוקס; born 10 April 1969) is an Israeli [major general](/wiki/Major_general "Major general") who commanded the [Central Command](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)") of the [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces") (IDF) until 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Previously, he commanded the [Gaza Division](/wiki/Gaza_Division "Gaza Division") of the (IDF).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Israeli major general (born 1969)

| Yehuda Fox | |

| Conflicts | * [South Lebanon conflict (1985–2000)](/wiki/South_Lebanon_conflict_(1985%E2%80%932000) "South Lebanon conflict (1985–2000)") * [First Intifada](/wiki/First_Intifada "First Intifada") * [Second Intifada](/wiki/Second_Intifada "Second Intifada") * [Operation Defensive Shield](/wiki/Operation_Defensive_Shield "Operation Defensive Shield") * [2006 Lebanon War](/wiki/2006_Lebanon_War "2006 Lebanon War") * [Operation Cast Lead](/wiki/Operation_Cast_Lead "Operation Cast Lead") * [Operation Pillar of Defense](/wiki/Operation_Pillar_of_Defense "Operation Pillar of Defense") * [Operation Protective Edge](/wiki/Operation_Protective_Edge "Operation Protective Edge") |

**Yehuda Fox** (also **Yehuda Fuchs**, [Hebrew](/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): יהודה פוקס; born 10 April 1969) is an Israeli [major general](/wiki/Major_general "Major general") who commanded the [Central Command](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)") of the [Israel Defense Forces](/wiki/Israel_Defense_Forces "Israel Defense Forces") (IDF) until 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Previously, he commanded the [Gaza Division](/wiki/Gaza_Division "Gaza Division") of the (IDF).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Military career

Fox is the son of Rabbi David Fox, a native of Chicago.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] His cousins are film director [Eytan Fox](/wiki/Eytan_Fox "Eytan Fox") and lawyer [David Fox](/wiki/David_Fox_(lawyer) "David Fox (lawyer)"). He was drafted into the IDF in 1987. He volunteered as a paratrooper in the [Paratroopers Brigade](/wiki/Paratroopers_Brigade "Paratroopers Brigade"). He served as a soldier and a [squad leader](/wiki/Squad_leader "Squad leader"). He became an [infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry") officer after completing [Officer Candidate School](/wiki/Officer_Candidate_School "Officer Candidate School").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Afterwards, he transferred to the [Nahal Brigade](/wiki/Nahal_Brigade "Nahal Brigade") and served as a [platoon leader](/wiki/Platoon_leader "Platoon leader") and as a [company commander](/wiki/Company_commander "Company commander"). Fox led the Brigade's [anti-tank](/wiki/Anti-tank "Anti-tank") company in [counter-guerrilla](/wiki/Counter-guerrilla "Counter-guerrilla") operations in [South Lebanon](/wiki/South_Lebanon_conflict_(1985%E2%80%932000) "South Lebanon conflict (1985–2000)"), commanded a Nahal battalion in [counter-terror](/wiki/Counter-terror "Counter-terror") operations in the [Second Intifada](/wiki/Second_Intifada "Second Intifada"). Later he commanded the [Judea Regional Brigade](/w/index.php?title=Judea_Regional_Brigade&action=edit&redlink=1 "Judea Regional Brigade (page does not exist)") (434th Territorial Brigade "Yehuda" of the [Judea and Samaria Division](/wiki/Judea_and_Samaria_Division "Judea and Samaria Division")), IDF's Officer Candidate School, and the Nahal Brigade. In 2014, he was appointed head of the [Paratroopers and Infantry Corps](/wiki/Infantry_Corps_(Israel) "Infantry Corps (Israel)"), and in 2016, commander of the [Gaza Division](/wiki/Gaza_Division "Gaza Division").

In 2019, Fox was appointed IDF's next [military attaché](/wiki/Military_attach%C3%A9 "Military attaché") in the United States.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In 2021, he replaced the outgoing commander of [Central Command](/wiki/Central_Command_(Israel) "Central Command (Israel)").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) In April 2024, Israeli media has announced he is stepping down from his position in the summer; however, it did not specify the reason behind Fox’s planned resignation, which was announced hours after [Aharon Haliva](/wiki/Aharon_Haliva "Aharon Haliva") resigned over his failure to predict the [Hamas attack](/wiki/October_7_attacks "October 7 attacks").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Retirement

In his retirement speech in July 2024, Fox described his feelings of guilt in the face of the army’s failure in preventing [October 7 massacre](/wiki/October_7_massacre "October 7 massacre"). He also criticised some of the [Israeli settler](/wiki/Israeli_settler "Israeli settler") leaders in the occupied [West Bank](/wiki/West_Bank "West Bank"):

:   “The great majority of settlers are law-abiding citizens who live in the shadow of the threat of terror... Unfortunately, in recent months, nationalist crime has reared its head under the cover of war and has led to revenge and sowed calamity and fear in Palestinian residents who do not pose any threat. To my dismay, the local leadership and the spiritual leadership for the most part did not see the threat as we did. It is intimidated and has not found the strength to come out openly and act in accordance with the values of Judaism... Even if the perpetrators are few in number, those who have remained silent have failed to isolate them and their actions from the majority. This isn’t Judaism in my eyes – at least it’s not the one I grew up with in my father’s and mother’s house.”[[6]](#cite_note-6)
